body.description: Returns the canonical greeting.
body.name: main

body.description: Look up a non-secret env entry by key. Raises `env_not_found` if the key is missing.
body.name: get

body.description: Look up a secret env entry by key. The result is the disjoint `secret` type and never leaks into `to_string` / f-strings. Raises `env_not_found` if the key is missing.
body.name: get_secret

body.description: Write an env entry. `is_secret = true` stores the value encrypted; reading it back requires `env.get_secret`.
body.name: set

body.description: Parse a JSON-encoded string into a `json` value. Raises `json_parse_error` on malformed input.
body.name: parse

body.description: Serialize a `json` value to canonical JSON text. The static type rules out closures / secrets / arbitrary tagged values, so this primitive is total (no runtime error path).
body.name: stringify

body.description: Divide two numbers (always returns number).
body.name: div

body.description: Absolute value (integer when the operand is integer).
body.name: abs

body.description: The smaller of two numbers (integer when both are integers).
body.name: min

body.description: The larger of two numbers (integer when both are integers).
body.name: max

body.description: Round down to the nearest integer.
body.name: floor

body.description: Round up to the nearest integer.
body.name: ceil

body.description: Round to the nearest integer (ties away from zero).
body.name: round

body.description: Construct an empty record.
body.name: empty

body.description: Retrieve a record entry by key. Returns null when the key is absent.
body.name: get

body.description: Insert or replace an entry, returning a fresh record (records are immutable).
body.name: set

body.description: Remove an entry by key. Returns the record unchanged when the key is absent.
body.name: remove

body.description: Unary negate.
body.name: neg

body.description: List the keys present in the record (insertion order is not guaranteed).
body.name: keys

body.description: True if the record carries an entry under the given key.
body.name: has

body.description: Number of entries in the record.
body.name: size

body.description: Number of Unicode code points in the string.
body.name: length

body.description: The sub-string from @start@ (inclusive) to @end@ (exclusive), counted in code points.
body.name: slice

body.description: True if @substring@ occurs anywhere in @value@.
body.name: contains

body.description: True if @value@ begins with @prefix@.
body.name: starts_with

body.description: True if @value@ ends with @suffix@.
body.name: ends_with

body.description: Code-point index of the first occurrence of @substring@, or -1 if absent.
body.name: index_of

body.description: Upper-case the string.
body.name: upper

body.description: Structural equality.
body.name: eq

body.description: Lower-case the string.
body.name: lower

body.description: Strip leading and trailing whitespace.
body.name: trim

body.description: Split on every occurrence of @separator@. An empty separator splits into code points.
body.name: split

body.description: Join the parts with @separator@ between them.
body.name: join

body.description: Replace every occurrence of @pattern@ with @replacement@ (literal, not a regex).
body.name: replace

body.description: Structural inequality.
body.name: ne

body.description: Less than.
body.name: lt

body.description: AI metadata of a callable (name / id / description / I-O schema).
body.name: agent_metadata

body.description: Less or equal.
body.name: le

body.description: Greater than.
body.name: gt

body.description: Greater or equal.
body.name: ge

body.description: Logical and.
body.name: and

body.description: Logical or.
body.name: or

body.description: Add two numbers.
body.name: add

body.description: Logical not.
body.name: not

body.description: Concatenate two strings (taint-aware: if either operand is secret, the result is secret).
body.name: concat

body.description: Return the runtime type-of-value tag as a string.
body.name: type_of

body.description: Serialize any value to its canonical wire-format string (Plan D — strings get quoted, tagged values get their @$constructor@ discriminator). Inverse: @from_string@.
body.name: to_string

body.description: Raised by @from_string@ when the input cannot be decoded as a canonical wire-format value (invalid JSON, illegal discriminator combinations, etc.). Handle via @request from_string_error(message) { ... }@.
body.name: from_string_error

body.description: Parse a wire-format string (produced by @to_string@) back into a value. Raises @from_string_error@ on malformed input.
body.name: from_string

body.description: Format a value for f-string interpolation. Accepts string or secret only (taint-aware: secret yields secret); other types must be converted via @to_string@ first.
body.name: format

body.description: Read a file's bytes back as a UTF-8 string.
body.name: file_to_string

body.description: Write a string's bytes to a new file (a persistent value reference). Rejects @secret@ (a credential must not be laundered into a re-readable file).
body.name: string_to_file

body.description: Subtract two numbers.
body.name: sub

body.description: Return the AI metadata of any callable value.
body.name: get_metadata

body.description: Raised by @call_agent@ when the name fails to resolve to a known callable, or the supplied args fail the target's input schema. Handle via @request call_agent_error(message) { ... }@.
body.name: call_agent_error

body.description: Dynamically invoke a callable by name. @name@ is the callable handle from @get_metadata@'s @id@: a qualified agent name (@module.agent@) or a closure ref (@closureref:<id>@); @args@ is a record matching the target's parameter list. Raises @call_agent_error@ on unknown names or schema-invalid args.
body.name: call_agent

body.description: Raise a recoverable runtime error. Bubbles through enclosing handle scopes until a `request throw` handler catches it; if nothing catches it the snapshot transitions to the `error` state.
body.name: throw

body.description: Raised when a requested env key is not present in the store. Handle to provide a default; if uncaught the snapshot transitions to the `error` state.
body.name: env_not_found

body.description: JSON null. The unit-shaped variant of the `json` union.
body.name: json_null

body.description: JSON boolean.
body.name: json_boolean

body.description: JSON integral number. Distinct from `json_number` so that round-trips through `parse` -> `stringify` preserve integer-vs-fractional form.
body.name: json_integer

body.description: JSON non-integral number.
body.name: json_number

body.description: JSON string.
body.name: json_string

body.description: JSON array of further `json` values.
body.name: json_array

body.description: JSON object: a record (homogeneous string-keyed map) of further `json` values.
body.name: json_object

body.description: Raised by `json.parse` when the input text is not valid JSON. Handle via `request json_parse_error(message) { ... }`.
body.name: json_parse_error

body.description: Multiply two numbers.
body.name: mul

body.description: Retrieve the element at @index@ (0-based). Raises out-of-bounds as a `throw`.
body.name: get

body.description: Number of elements in the array.
body.name: length

body.description: The empty array. Its element type is `never` (the empty array has no elements), which is a subtype of every `array[T]`, so it can seed a typed accumulator: `var history = array.empty()` grown with `array.append`.
body.name: empty

body.description: A single-element array carrying @value@.
body.name: of

body.description: Append @value@ to the end, returning a fresh array (arrays are immutable).
body.name: append

body.description: Floor-mod two numbers.
body.name: mod

body.description: Concatenate two arrays into a fresh array.
body.name: concat

body.description: The sub-array from @start@ (inclusive) to @end@ (exclusive), 0-based.
body.name: slice

body.description: The array reversed.
body.name: reverse

body.description: True if @value@ is structurally equal to some element.
body.name: contains

body.description: Index of the first element structurally equal to @value@, or -1 if absent.
body.name: index_of
